metrics.narrative: Salesforce's Business_Objectives__c lays out a detailed qualitative case: reducing manual intervention across S2C and P2P, improving auditability and cycle times, and supporting fleet modernization and network growth. On the 19 Jan 2026 discovery call Astrid Collado-Tanquieng named the specific Phase 1 pain (manual, non-PO reimbursement processing with no real-time status tracking) that the buyer wants automated first. No dollar figure, cycle-time baseline, or ROI number has been captured from the buyer to date, so the business case remains directional rather than quantified.

champion.narrative: Salesforce names Anna Pamela Mollasgo-Dela Pasion (Director Procurement) as champion (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c) and she also holds the Decision Maker contact role. She personally emailed Carl Kimball on 9 Jul 2026 to arrange a lunch meeting after the ProcureCon plenary, and the rep's same-day note describes her as having 'strong influence' and being 'in control of voting,' while cautioning that she 'will need to be sensitive in any preference for us internally' given Coupa's aggressive push. This is direct, buyer-initiated advocacy from a person with real voting power, though her position requires careful, low-key management while the competitive bake-off is live.

competition.narrative: Coupa is the dominant, recurring threat: named as one of '3 in race' on 10 Feb 2026, 'still in the mix' on 26 May 2026, and per the rep's 9 Jul 2026 note, 'pushing hard and overpricing' during the current RFP cycle. SAP Ariba appears only in the static Competitors__c CRM field with no corroborating call or note evidence in any period reviewed, so it reads as a stale or background entry rather than an active current threat.

identify_pain.narrative: Salesforce's Customer_Business_Problem__c states Cebu Pacific runs SAP ERP 'with a lot of manual intervention,' and Business_Objectives__c details fragmented S2C and P2P execution, weak contract-to-spend traceability, and elevated operational risk tied to fleet and network growth plans. On the 19 Jan 2026 call, Astrid Collado-Tanquieng articulated the specific driving pain: a highly manual, non-PO reimbursement process with no real-time visibility, which the buyer wants automated before addressing upstream sourcing.

paper_process.narrative: A revised NDA 'with complete details' was requested by the buyer on 21 Mar 2026, and no source reviewed (Next Step, Next Step History, Tasks, or Avoma) confirms it was delivered or executed. No MSA, SOW, or legal review has started. Anne Tieng (VP Legal Affairs) attended a Zycus/Cebu Pacific meeting on 22 May 2026, but no contracting activity has followed. With the close date at 30 Jul 2026, the paper process has effectively not begun.

economic_buyer.narrative: Salesforce marks the sponsor 'to be identified' (X10a_Sponsor__c) and Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c is false. Mark Cezar (CFO) holds an Influencer contact role but has never attended a Zycus call or meeting in any source reviewed. On 9 Jul 2026 Rohan Kapoor told the rep 'budget at exco level, and project is green light,' meaning approval authority sits with an executive committee rather than one named buyer, and the rep's own 8 Jul 2026 note says he expects to secure a direct CFO meeting 'during the RFP period, 2-3 weeks.' Access to power today is indirect, routed through Rohan Kapoor and the exco process, not yet confirmed with a named economic buyer.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer ran a structured RFI (Letter of Invitation released 30 Jan 2026, LOI/reviewed MNDA due 13 Feb 2026), followed by vendor demos, and has now moved into an RFP that Astrid says went out 'this week' (as of 8 Jul 2026). Rohan Kapoor confirmed on 9 Jul 2026 that internal management approvals are underway and that Anna Pamela Mollasgo-Dela Pasion 'is in control of voting.' The stage sequence is clear, but the formal approval chain beyond Anna Pamela's vote and the executive committee has not been fully mapped.

decision_criteria.narrative: Business_Requirements__c is blank in Salesforce (a hygiene gap, not a knowledge gap), but the 19 Jan 2026 discovery call laid out concrete evaluation criteria directly from Astrid Collado-Tanquieng: seamless SAP integration via API without recreating POs in Zycus, support for both SAP ECC and HANA, hybrid PO scenarios (POs originating in either system), automated rules-based matching for low-value/low-risk invoices, and a user-friendly portal for non-PO payment requests. A Zycus product specialist on that call also probed UX and change-management importance directly with Astrid.

contributions.detail: Close date (Jul 30) sits only ~3 weeks after the buyer's own RFP requirements were still described as 'coming' (Jul 9) — the timeline is not defensible against a live RFP process. ► Push the close date to reflect the real evaluation/RFP schedule.
No Economic Buyer identified or engaged — Rohan (CPO) and Pam (Dir. Procurement) are the sole named contacts; CFO access remains aspirational.
Coupa is actively 'pushing hard' on price per the buyer's own team, and the champion (Pam) must manage internal sensitivity around her preference — real competitive and internal-politics exposure.
Funding was explicitly paused in May ('project will slow due to funding caution') — even though reportedly resolved by July, the volatility itself signals budget fragility.
The one bright spot: exco-level budget sign-off reported Jul 9 removes what had been the single biggest risk (funding), though it is not yet corroborated by a paper trail.

flags: Single-threaded: Rohan is sole senior buyer contact; no CFO (Mark Cezar) or C-level engagement in Next Step or tasks despite 24-day close
Champion weak and at risk: Rohan stated project slowing May 26, no confirmed response to rep outreach June 6/22, July 8; engagement cooling

Run competitive review with champion Rohan before ProcureCon (July 8) — Coupa has been in the race 189 days and is still 'in the mix' despite Zycus being 'clear favored vendor.' No formal down-select or head-to-head session documented. Surface Coupa's weaknesses: (1) portal lock-in (supplier must register/pay fees vs Zycus's inclusive email/eInvoice channels), (2) SAP integration complexity (Coupa requires PO recreation vs Zycus API-based hybrid PO with official ECC + S/4 adapters), (3) higher TCO (Coupa SaaS + Coupa Pay fees vs Zycus all-in licensing). Arm Rohan with the differentiation case to defend Zycus to the CFO and eliminate Coupa before they move on commercials.

Close date July 30 is indefensible: 22 days to close, still in Formal Evaluation, no EB, no budget, no pricing, no contract. Required progression through 4 stages (Shortlisted, Vendor Selected, Negotiation, Contract In Progress) plus all commercial, legal, and InfoSec gates is unrealistic. Recommended forecast: Pipeline.

No agreed pricing or commercials. May 26 'currently reviewing a new proposal to submit' for smaller scope implies pricing not finalized. No discount, term length, payment milestones, or commercial proposal acceptance documented 23 days from close.

No contracting started: Revised NDA requested March 21 with no confirmation of delivery or signature. No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal review, or InfoSec gate documented. VP Legal Anne Tieng and IT Compliance Manager Katrina Reynoso are contact roles but no legal or InfoSec workstream is active. 22 days to close with no contract is fatal.

Cebu Pacific's SAP-heavy stack is internally flagged as a reason to stay in-ecosystem (Why_not_Zycus__c); lead with the ECC/HANA adapter proof shown in the 19 Jan 2026 demo to neutralize the SAP Ariba path.
Get the outstanding NDA (requested 21 Mar 2026) confirmed and move early legal/paper-process groundwork in parallel with the RFP evaluation so contracting does not become the bottleneck if selected.

No formal down-select or head-to-head session documented. Arm Rohan with the differentiation case to defend Zycus to the CFO and eliminate Coupa before they move on commercials.
